content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `453f57bf5a70cd69f9a3fb0345fadc4bcf80f6fb` and found 2 fix-now issues, both around trusting or describing the new `Nearby work` section.

## Amendments

1. Contract-marked prompt overrides can now invent `## Nearby work` IDs that review and final-review treat as valid `mapped_to_bead_id` owners. `create_project_from_bead_context` only shape-validates overrides ([src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-432]); later stages derive the planned-elsewhere allowlist directly from prompt text ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195], [src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:467-530, 2382-2385], [src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:591-632]). A stale or hand-written override can therefore silently reroute findings to beads that were never graph-derived for this task.
   Rationale: `Nearby work` is supposed to be builder-owned graph context, but the new allowlist trusts any contract-marked override. That can suppress legitimate fix-now findings or map them to the wrong bead.
2. Prompt-review guidance becomes self-contradictory on malformed contract-marked prompts that do not actually contain `## Nearby work`. When validation fails, `prompt_authoritative_section_titles()` falls back to the full current section list ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:197-210]), and `prompt_review_consumer_guidance_for_prompt()` then tells the refiner to preserve the graph-derived nearby-work body verbatim ([src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:284-300, 338-342]). The validator later rejects any refinement that adds `## Nearby work` when the original prompt lacked it ([src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-370]), so drift-recovery can churn on impossible instructions.
   Rationale: This affects the exact recovery path for contract-marked prompts that are already malformed: the refiner is told to preserve/add a section that the validator forbids, which can waste retries or block repair.
